Android-based operating system for Amazon devices

This article is about the Android-based operating system by Amazon. For the HTML5-powered operating system by Mozilla, see [Firefox OS](/wiki/Firefox_OS "Firefox OS").

**Fire OS** is an [Android (AOSP)](/wiki/Android_(operating_system)#AOSP "Android (operating system)")-based [operating system](/wiki/Operating_system "Operating system") developed by [Amazon](/wiki/Amazon_(company) "Amazon (company)") for their hardware [devices](#Devices). Fire OS includes a customized [user interface](/wiki/Graphical_user_interface "Graphical user interface") primarily centered on content consumption, and heavy ties to content available from Amazon's storefronts including [Appstore](/wiki/Amazon_Appstore "Amazon Appstore") and services.[[10]](#cite_note-10)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fire_OS&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

See also: [Fire HD § Software](/wiki/Fire_HD#Software "Fire HD")

Amazon began referring to the Android derivative as Fire OS with its third iteration of [Fire tablets](/wiki/Amazon_Fire "Amazon Fire"). Unlike previous Fire models, whose operating system was described as "based on" Android, Fire OS 3.0 was described as "compatible with" Android.[[11]](#cite_note-auto-11)

### Fire OS 5

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fire_OS&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Fire OS 5")]

Based on [Android 5.1](/wiki/Android_Lollipop "Android Lollipop") "Lollipop",[[12]](#cite_note-:0-12) it added an updated interface. The home screen has a traditional application grid and pages for content types, as opposed to the previous carousel interface. It also introduced On Deck, a function that automatically moves content out of offline storage to maintain storage space for new content; the Word Runner [speed reading](/wiki/Speed_reading "Speed reading") tool; and screen color filters. Parental controls were enhanced with a new web browser for FreeTime mode featuring a curated selection of content appropriate for children, and an Activity Center for monitoring children's usage.[[13]](#cite_note-pl-fireos5-13)[[14]](#cite_note-fortune-raremisfire-14)[[15]](#cite_note-pcworld-kidstablet-15) It removed support for device encryption, which an Amazon spokesperson stated was an enterprise-oriented feature that was underused. In March 2016, after the removal was publicized and criticized in the wake of the [FBI–Apple encryption dispute](/wiki/FBI%E2%80%93Apple_encryption_dispute "FBI–Apple encryption dispute"), Amazon announced it would restore the feature in a future patch.[[16]](#cite_note-verge-removed-16)[[17]](#cite_note-verge-nodeviceencryption-17)

### Fire OS 6

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fire_OS&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Fire OS 6")]

Based on [Android 7.1.2](/wiki/Android_Nougat "Android Nougat") "Nougat", its main changes and additions include:[[18]](#cite_note-:1-18)

* Adoptable storage, allowing users to format and use their [SD card](/wiki/SD_card "SD card") as internal storage
* Doze/App standby, aiming to improve battery life by forcing devices to sleep when not actively used, adding restrictions to apps that would normally continue to run background processes[[19]](#cite_note-19)

### MediaTek exploits (2019)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fire_OS&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: MediaTek exploits (2019)")]

In early 2019, [security exploits](/wiki/Exploit_(computer_security) "Exploit (computer security)") for six [Fire Tablet](/wiki/Amazon_Fire "Amazon Fire") models and one [Fire TV](/wiki/Amazon_Fire_TV "Amazon Fire TV") model[[20]](#cite_note-20) were discovered that could allow temporary [root access](/wiki/Superuser "Superuser"), permanent root access, and bootloader unlocking[[a]](#cite_note-21)[[21]](#cite_note-22)[[22]](#cite_note-23) due to [security vulnerabilities](/wiki/Vulnerability_(computing) "Vulnerability (computing)") in multiple [MediaTek](/wiki/MediaTek "MediaTek") chipsets.[[23]](#cite_note-24)[[24]](#cite_note-25)[[25]](#cite_note-26)

### Fire OS 7

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fire_OS&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Fire OS 7")]

Based on [Android 9](/wiki/Android_Pie "Android Pie") "Pie",[[26]](#cite_note-27) it was released in 2019 for all 8th-11th generation Fire tablets.

### Fire OS 8

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fire_OS&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Fire OS 8")]

FireOS 8 is the latest release of FireOS for 12th-13th generation Fire tablets, based on [Android 11](/wiki/Android_11 "Android 11"), information about the release became available via Amazon developer documentation around May 2022.

FireOS 8 incorporates changes from Android 10 and Android 11, such as TLS 1.3 support enabled by default, High Efficiency Image File Format (HEIF) support, Dark mode, One-time permissions, Sharing improvements, Device auto backups (user needs to opt-in to device backups), etc.[[27]](#cite_note-28)

Although it is noted in the Amazon developer documentation that some Android 11 features such as File Based Encryption (FBE) are not supported yet.[[28]](#cite_note-:2-29)

Fire OS will continue to be supported on existing Amazon Fire TV devices and will continue to be used on newer devices.[[29]](#cite_note-30)

### Fire OS 14

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fire_OS&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Fire OS 14")]

FireOS 14 is based on [Android 14](/wiki/Android_14 "Android 14"). Development information was made available on Amazon developer documentation in 2025.[[30]](#cite_note-31)

## Features

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fire_OS&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Features")]

Fire OS is based on the Android Open Source Project (AOSP). It does not come with [Google mobile services](/wiki/Google_Mobile_Services "Google Mobile Services") pre-installed; therefore, Amazon cannot use the Android trademarks to market the devices.[[3]](#cite_note-ars-irongrip-3) Users are able to [sideload](/wiki/Sideloading "Sideloading") the [Google Play store](/wiki/Google_Play "Google Play");[[31]](#cite_note-32) however, full compatibility is not guaranteed if the app depends on Google services.[[32]](#cite_note-33)

Because [Google](/wiki/Google "Google") services are not pre-installed, Amazon develops and uses its own apps in their place, some of which include [Amazon Appstore](/wiki/Amazon_Appstore "Amazon Appstore"), [Amazon Alexa](/wiki/Amazon_Alexa "Amazon Alexa"), [Prime Video](/wiki/Amazon_Video "Amazon Video"), [Amazon Music](/wiki/Amazon_MP3 "Amazon MP3"), [Audible](/wiki/Audible.com "Audible.com"), [Kindle Store](/wiki/Kindle_Store "Kindle Store"), [Silk Browser](/wiki/Amazon_Silk "Amazon Silk"), [Goodreads](/wiki/Goodreads "Goodreads") and [Here WeGo](/wiki/Here_WeGo "Here WeGo").[[33]](#cite_note-34)[[34]](#cite_note-35)

Fire OS uses a customized home screen ([launcher](/wiki/List_of_Android_launchers "List of Android launchers")). As of Fire OS 7.3.2.3, the launcher features three sections:[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

* "For You" shows the weather, recently used apps, Alexa integration, then shows recommended content such as apps, books movies, etc.
* "Home" is the section for the icons of all of the apps currently installed on the device, apps on the Home section can be moved around or put into folders, a search bar is also available at the top of the launcher to search though local content on the device or search online using the [Bing](/wiki/Microsoft_Bing "Microsoft Bing") [search engine](/wiki/Search_engine "Search engine").
* "Library" shows purchased items from Amazon services, such as apps, books, movies and TV shows from Prime Video, etc.

The OS features a [multi-user](/wiki/Multi-user_software "Multi-user software") system, which allows multiple people to set up and use separate user profiles.[[35]](#cite_note-36)

Along with Amazon Kids and Amazon Kids+, a suite of [parental controls](/wiki/Parental_controls "Parental controls") is included which allows parents to create managed child profiles,[[36]](#cite_note-37) set limits and set restrictions for minors.[[37]](#cite_note-pcmag-freetime-38)[[38]](#cite_note-39)

## Devices

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fire_OS&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Devices")]

Current Amazon devices running Fire OS:[[11]](#cite_note-auto-11)

* [Fire Tablets](/wiki/Amazon_Fire "Amazon Fire")
* [Fire TV](/wiki/Amazon_Fire_TV "Amazon Fire TV")
* [Amazon Echo](/wiki/Amazon_Echo "Amazon Echo") / [Amazon Echo Show](/wiki/Amazon_Echo_Show "Amazon Echo Show")

Discontinued devices running Fire OS:

* [Fire Phone](/wiki/Fire_Phone "Fire Phone")[[39]](#cite_note-40)

## Vega OS

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fire_OS&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: Vega OS")]

In November 2023, a report surfaced of Amazon's plans to migrate away from Android to a custom [Linux](/wiki/Linux "Linux")-based [operating system](/wiki/Operating_system "Operating system") known as Vega OS with plans to launch TVs running Vega in 2024.[[50]](#cite_note-52) Amazon was reported to have been developing the operating system since 2017.[[51]](#cite_note-53)

On September 30, 2025, Amazon officially announced Vega OS, which will be used on newer Fire TV devices, beginning with the [Amazon Fire TV 4K Select](/wiki/Amazon_Fire_TV#Fire_TV_Stick_4K_Select "Amazon Fire TV").[[52]](#cite_note-54)[[53]](#cite_note-55) Apps will be [HTML5](/wiki/HTML5 "HTML5") based, specifically using [React Native](/wiki/React_Native "React Native").

* [Sailfish OS](/wiki/Sailfish_OS "Sailfish OS"), a [Linux](/wiki/Linux "Linux")-based mobile OS by [Jolla](/wiki/Jolla "Jolla") which includes an Android runtime
* [BlackBerry 10](/wiki/BlackBerry_10 "BlackBerry 10"), a [QNX](/wiki/QNX "QNX")-based mobile OS by [BlackBerry](/wiki/BlackBerry "BlackBerry") which includes an Android runtime and comes with the [Amazon Appstore](/wiki/Amazon_Appstore "Amazon Appstore") preloaded
* [Comparison of mobile operating systems](/wiki/Comparison_of_mobile_operating_systems "Comparison of mobile operating systems")
